name: Cambridge Dawn
oneLiner: The UK's fastest AI supercomputer and the other half of the AIRR, now expanding six-fold.
summary: Dawn is the UK's fastest AI supercomputer and, with Isambard-AI, forms the AI Research Resource. Government funding is expanding its capacity six-fold by spring 2026. The system comprises 256 Dell PowerEdge XE9640 servers, each with two Intel Xeon Platinum 8468 processors, 1 TiB of RAM, and four Intel Data Center GPU Max 1550 GPUs linked by Xe-Link and HDR200 InfiniBand. A near-term upgrade adds AMD MI355X GPUs with StackHPC software to diversify the national AI compute supply chain.

keyFacts.hardware: 256 Dell PowerEdge XE9640 nodes with Intel Xeon Platinum 8468 CPUs and Intel GPU Max 1550 GPUs; AMD MI355X upgrade planned
keyFacts.energy: Run within Cambridge Research Computing's managed, federated operations model

access.route: Free access via AIRR allocations, with project administration through the Cambridge Research Computing Portal.

useCases: Large language model inference with high-bandwidth memory GPUs

sources.label: CSD3 documentation - Dawn Intel GPU nodes
